text: The problem of development of social competence of high school students in the context of non-formal education The object of research is an issue of development of social competence of high school students in the context of non-formal education. The subject – development of social competence of high school students in the context of non-formal education. Research experiment included 647 high school students from seven Ukrainian secondary comprehensive schools of Ternopil and Ivano-Frankivsk regions. Diagnostic methods specified with key components of social competence structure have been used for recording of initial state of development of social competence of high school students at pre-experiment stage. Correspondent criteria, indicators and descriptive scale of levels of formation were introduced and characterized. Findings of diagnostic survey show considerable lack of social competence of a future school graduate. The core socio-pedagogical conditions, according to the main provisions of non-formal education, were established during the experiment (from September 2017 until June 2019). They involved stimulation of a subjective position of a student, socio-pedagogical support and control, modernisation of content, forms and methods of non-formal education, creation of an amateur space and support. The diversity of forms and methods of realisation of non-formal education in the experimental groups of students has led to a noticeable positive change in development of their social competence. According to the results of the comparative correlation analysis of post-experiment data (which proved the scientific fact), the increase in the number of students with a high level of social competence made up to 15% and decrease in the number of respondents with a low level of social competence was 11%. Thus, the final analysis of the obtained data generally confirmed the hypothesis of the research, having proved the importance of the shift of the main attention from formal to non-formal education type for development of social competence of high school students.
